data.title: Forget Rockets — SpaceX Takes $20 Billion Telecom Giant Public
data.description:
Everyone's going to call it the biggest initial public offering ever. They'll talk about rockets, Mars and Elon Musk. But they'll be looking at the wrong business. That’s because the SpaceX IPO isn't really about launch vehicles.

It's about Starlink—a satellite internet network that has quietly become one of the fastest-growing telecom businesses in the world.

And it's growing like a startup.

Starlink Growth Engine

That's what makes this IPO interesting.

Valuation Debate Shift

At a headline valuation of about $1.75 trillion, the instinct is to compare SpaceX to aerospace peers. But that framework breaks down quickly. Rockets are capital-intensive and cyclical.

Starlink is recurring, subscription-driven, and scaling fast.

The better comparison might be early Amazon.com, Inc‘s (NASDAQ:AMZN) AWS—a high-growth infrastructure layer that looked expensive until it wasn't.

Which raises the real question: is the market about to price SpaceX like a space company—or like a hypergrowth telecom platform?

Because if it's the latter, the valuation math looks very different.

And the biggest IPO in history may end up being something else entirely—a telecom story hiding behind a rocket narrative.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: Anavex Life Sciences Stock Crushed As European Alzheimer's Drug Filing Pulled After EMA Pushback
data.description:
This decision follows feedback from the European Medicines Agency’s (EMA) Committee for Medicinal Products for Human Use (CHMP), which indicated it would not issue a positive opinion at this time.

European Thumbs Down For Anavex Lead Drug

Anavex had submitted the application following encouragement by the SME Office of the EMA in October 2023, when the EMA representatives encouraged Anavex “to immediately begin preparing” for the application and that the “next step for Anavex in terms of interactions with EMA would be to apply for a marketing authorization application.”

Anavex now has to take note of the differing assessment of the CHMP at the EMA and will consider the feedback received from the CHMP and focus on gathering additional data and conducting further analyses to address the points raised by the CHMP.

In November 2025, EMA’s CHMP adopted a negative trend vote for blarcamesine.

In December, CHMP adopted a negative opinion on the marketing authorization application for blarcamesine.

Anavex requested from the EMA the re-examination in December 2025.

Alzheimer's Program Update

During a Type C meeting with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) in January, Anavex Life Sciences discussed the potential pathways to support an NDA (New Drug Application) for Alzheimer's disease.

The company provided information related to the scientific rationale and profile of blarcamesine as an oral administration, being convenient to dispense, and the absence of significant safety concerns in clinical trials of blarcamesine conducted so far, extending to the lack of amyloid-related imaging abnormalities.

AVXL Stock Price Activity: Anavex Life Sciences shares were down 32.69% at $2.82 at the time of publication on Wednesday. The stock is trading at a new 52-week low, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image via Shutterstock

data.title: Why Are Coeur Mining Shares Trading Higher On Wednesday?
data.description:
This news comes as the broader market experienced gains on Tuesday, with the S&P 500 rising 0.83%, indicating a favorable environment for the stock.

Details

Coeur Mining has commenced a private exchange offer for any and all of the $40 million aggregate principal amount of the 6.875% Senior Notes due 2032.

This move is part of a strategy to streamline its debt obligations and is accompanied by a consent solicitation to amend the indenture governing the existing notes.

This strategic maneuver follows Coeur’s recent acquisition of New Gold Inc. on March 20. This has implications for its debt management and future financial flexibility.

Post-Acquisition Guidance

The company expects 2026 consolidated production of 680,000–815,000 ounces of gold, 18.7–21.9 million ounces of silver, and 50–65 million pounds of copper. This includes nine months of output from its two new Canadian mines.

The Board also approved an expanded $750 million share repurchase program. Also, the company introduced a semiannual dividend of $0.02 per share, expected in June and December.

Coeur has also secured a new $1.0 billion revolving credit facility to enhance liquidity.

Technical Analysis

The broader market experienced gains on Tuesday, with the Technology sector rising 1.05%. Coeur’s stock performance aligns with this positive market sentiment, suggesting that investor confidence may be bolstered by the company’s proactive financial strategies.

Currently, the stock is trading 12.1% below its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 0.7% below its 100-day SMA, indicating some short-term weakness. Over the past 12 months, shares have surged 188.35%, reflecting a strong longer-term trend, and are currently positioned closer to their 52-week highs than lows.

Earnings & Analyst Outlook

Coeur Mining is slated to provide its next financial update on May 6, 2026 (estimated).

  • EPS Estimate: 52 cents (Up from 11 cents)
  • Revenue Estimate: $730.70 million (Up from $360.00 million)
  • Valuation: P/E of 19.3x (Indicates fair valuation)

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Buy Rating with an average price target of $14.59. Recent analyst moves include:

  • CIBC: Initiated with Outperformer (Target $40.00) (Mar. 10)
  • Canaccord Genuity: Downgraded to Hold (Lowers Target to $26.00) (Feb. 20)
  • RBC Capital: Outperform (Raises Target to $26.00) (Feb. 12)

## Section: Benzinga Edge Rankings

Below is the Benzinga Edge scorecard for Coeur Mining, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses compared to the broader market:

  • Value Rank: 59.5 — Indicates moderate value positioning.
  • Growth Rank: 99.22 — Suggests strong growth potential.
  • Momentum Rank: 95.53 — Indicates strong momentum in price performance.

The Verdict: Coeur Mining’s Benzinga Edge signal reveals a growth-heavy profile, characterized by strong momentum and growth rankings, suggesting that the stock may continue to attract investor interest as it navigates its strategic initiatives.

Top ETF Exposure

Significance: Because CDE carries such a heavy weight in these funds, any significant inflows or outflows for these ETFs will likely force automatic buying or selling of the stock.

CDE Stock Price Activity: Coeur Mining shares were up 6.37% at $19.48 during premarket trading on Wednesday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Photo via Shutterstock

data.title: Beam Therapeutics Gains On Clinical Update As Gene Therapy Hits Protective Protein Levels
data.description:
BEAM-302 is Beam’s lead genetic disease program for liver-targeting therapy for alpha-1 antitrypsin deficiency (AATD) that addresses the underlying pathophysiology of both liver and lung disease.

AATD is a hereditary disorder caused by low levels of protective AAT protein, leading to lung damage (emphysema, COPD) and liver disease.

Data Show AAT Levels Above Protective Threshold

The company announced updated safety and efficacy data from its ongoing Phase 1/2 trial of BEAM-302, highlighting that treatment with 60 mg of the drug led to a mean steady-state total alpha-1 antitrypsin (AAT) level of 16.1 µM.

Notably, all patients remained consistently and durably above the 11 µM protective AAT threshold, with a significant reduction in mutant Z-AAT levels.

Additionally, the trial demonstrated a well-tolerated safety profile, with no serious adverse events reported.

The company plans to initiate a global pivotal cohort in the second half of 2026, further advancing its efforts in treating alpha-1 antitrypsin deficiency (AATD).

BEAM-302 Pivotal Development

Based on feedback from the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), Beam intends to pursue an accelerated approval pathway for BEAM-302 based on a primary endpoint of AAT biomarkers evaluated over 12 months, with 60 mg as the selected dose.

Beam expects to initiate the pivotal cohort in the second half of 2026.

Technical Indicators Reflect Mixed Momentum

Currently, Beam Therapeutics is trading 7.5% below its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 8.7% below its 100-day SMA, indicating some short-term weakness.

Over the past 12 months, shares have increased by 3.14% and are currently positioned closer to their 52-week highs than lows.

The RSI is at 38.84, which is considered neutral territory, while the MACD is at -0.9269, below its signal line at -0.7546, indicating bearish pressure on the stock. The combination of neutral RSI and bearish MACD suggests mixed momentum.

  • Key Resistance: $29.00
  • Key Support: $23.50

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Buy Rating with an average price target of $47.57. Recent analyst moves include:

  • RBC Capital: Sector Perform (Raises Target to $26.00) (Feb. 25)
  • Wedbush: Outperform (Raises Target to $65.00) (Feb. 25)
  • Canaccord Genuity: Initiated with Buy (Target $74.00) (Feb. 20)

Top ETF Exposure

  • ARK Innovation ETF (NYSE:ARKK): 3.60% Weight
  • ARK Genomic Revolution ETF (BATS:ARKG): 4.96% Weight

BEAM Stock Price Activity: Beam Therapeutics shares were up 2.71% at $24.60 during premarket trading on Wednesday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image via Shutterstock

data.title: Trade Desk Stock Stalls As Investors Weigh Publicis Fallout And AI Risks
data.description:
• Trade Desk stock is building positive momentum. What’s driving TTD shares up?

Publicis Fallout Pressures Confidence In The Trade Desk

The stock remains under pressure following reports from last week that Publicis Groupe advised clients to stop using The Trade Desk after a third-party audit reportedly raised concerns about fee practices and transparency.

That development appeared to shake confidence in one of the company's core selling points: trust with advertisers and agencies using its programmatic ad-buying platform.

AI Disruption Fears Add To Pressure On Software Stocks

A Reuters report Tuesday also suggested those fears are contributing to investor unease in private markets, reinforcing the idea that AI disruption is becoming a more serious valuation issue across tech.

The Trade Desk Faces A Tougher Growth Narrative

For The Trade Desk, that creates a difficult backdrop. The company is not only facing fallout from the recent agency-related controversy, but also a broader market environment in which investors are reassessing whether ad-tech and SaaS players can maintain pricing power, differentiation and long-term growth as AI capabilities expand.

Recent Momentum Looks Choppy And Range-Bound

The RSI has spent most of the past year in the neutral range, with occasional spikes into overbought territory above 70 and several dips into oversold levels near 30.

Recently, momentum appears choppy and range-bound, with repeated swings between the mid-20s and mid-50s, suggesting a lack of sustained directional strength.

Analyst Sentiment Has Turned More Cautious

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Buy Rating with an average price target of $40.43. Recent analyst moves include:

  • Evercore ISI Group: Outperform (Lowers Target to $32.00) (Mar. 19)
  • Rosenblatt: Downgraded to Neutral (Lowers Target to $25.00) (Mar. 18)
  • Stifel: Downgraded to Hold (Lowers Target to $26.00) (Mar. 18)

TTD Shares Trade Flat Wednesday Morning

TTD Price Action: Trade Desk shares were down 0.40% at $22.25 at the time of publication on Wednesday. The stock is trading near its 52-week low of $21.08, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image: Shutterstock
